title: Market Brief — Tuesday, May 26, 2026
summary: GPU supply is loosening fast across the stack. Util-avail stands at 25% for A100, 36% for H100, 54% for H200, and 25% for B200, with H100 and H200 shedding 24pp and 31pp of utilization in 30 days respectively. H100 spot jumped 10.1% day-over-day to $2.91/hr, a counter-trend squeeze amid collapsing demand that signals short-covering, not organic buying.
paragraphs: GPU supply is loosening fast across the stack. Util-avail stands at 25% for A100, 36% for H100, 54% for H200, and 25% for B200, with H100 and H200 shedding 24pp and 31pp of utilization in 30 days respectively. H100 spot jumped 10.1% day-over-day to $2.91/hr, a counter-trend squeeze amid collapsing demand that signals short-covering, not organic buying.

At TCO-adjusted cost of $10.64/M tokens on H100, anyone selling Llama 70B inference at medium tier is burning roughly $12.64/M tokens per call. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.45/M output and Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M output are priced far below any hardware cost floor, subsidizing adoption with venture dollars. Grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Neutral, Google Strong, DeepSeek Burning Cash, Meta Burning Cash.

B200 is undervalued at $5.13/hr spot with the best inference margin profile, turning positive at high tier where H100 and H200 still bleed. Short H200 exposure where 54% util-avail signals emerging oversupply and vol runs 194% annualized, making hedging prohibitively expensive.
bullets: 54% util-avail on H200 marks the loosest supply of any GPU class, approaching oversupply territory.
25% util-avail on B200 remains the tightest alongside A100, signaling sustained demand for newest and cheapest-per-token silicon.
21.78 on the LLMflation Index means frontier output tokens cost 78% less than GPT-4 did at March 2023 launch.
$80/M output for o3 Pro makes it 222x more expensive than OpenAI's own gpt-oss-120b at $0.18/M.
193.5% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI is 4x that of A100, making H200 the riskiest compute position to hold.
$3.75/M TCO-adjusted cost on B200 undercuts H100 by 56%, the widest efficiency gap in the current stack.
